mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, reducing recession risk and supporting equity valuations through higher expected corporate earnings and consumer spending. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive 20-day forward returns when PAYEMS shows consistent month-over-month growth.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 133852.0 thousand as of Nov 2012 reflects post-crisis recovery momentum
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS growth fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25% for consecutive months, the zero lower bound constraint signals extended monetary accommodation. This threshold-triggered regime produces positive equity returns as low rates compress discount rates and support asset price inflation. The mechanism activates only when FEDFUNDS stays pinned near zero.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of Nov 2012 confirms zero lower bound regime
regime_hint: post-crisis zero lower bound monetary policy
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or correlation between FEDFUNDS level and forward returns becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) combined with accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) signals strengthening aggregate demand and consumer confidence. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive 20-day returns when both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, reflecting joint validation of demand-side momentum that equity markets reward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 894.0 thousand and RSAFS at 412403.0 million as of Oct-Nov 2012
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand recovery
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in HOUST and RSAFS fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness or demand destruction. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative 5-day forward returns when INDPRO falls, as markets interpret production declines as early recession signals that compress near-term equity valuations.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to produce negative 5d returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 8.0% signals labor market deterioration and increased recession risk. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative 60-day forward returns when unemployment crosses above this threshold, as equity markets price in lower corporate earnings and consumer spending contraction.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.7% as of Nov 2012, near post-crisis lows
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: UNRATE > 8.0% fails to produce negative 60d returns in > 55% of instances, or correlation with forward returns becomes positive and significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 2.5% year-over-year combined with FEDFUNDS remaining below 0.5% creates a real rate compression that supports equity valuations through negative real discount rates. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive 20-day returns when inflation accelerates while monetary policy stays accommodative.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 231.025 and FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of Nov 2012
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: Joint condition fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in multivariate model.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly shows sustained expansion above 2.0% annualized rate, signaling healthy economic fundamentals that support equity earnings growth. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive 60-day forward returns when GDP growth remains above trend, reflecting market confidence in corporate profit sustainability.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13638.1 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q3 2012
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDP growth fails to produce positive 60d returns in > 58% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) declining month-over-month signals weakening consumer demand and potential earnings headwinds for equities. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative 20-day forward returns when RSAFS falls, as markets interpret consumer spending weakness as a leading indicator of profit margin compression.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 412403.0 million as of Nov 2012
failure_condition: RSAFS declines fail to produce negative 20d returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
